content: Just as human language learning explicitly teaches the spelling and conceptual definition of 'reflexion' as a core cognitive act of reflecting, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly grounding the agent's self-reflection memory in standardized lexical concept anchors of 'reflexion' drawn from educational curricula. This creates a structured schema for the LLM to organize inconsistent self-evaluation perspectives instead of treating self-reflection as an unstructured emergent ability.
